purpose: Fix joinParty() 'Missing or insufficient permissions' error
Fix isPartyMember() to correctly verify membership via subcollection
Clean up dead members map code
non_goals: Not adding new features to party system

tech_stack.name: Firebase Firestore Security Rules
tech_stack.usage_context: Access control for all Firestore collections

tech_stack.name: Firebase Firestore
tech_stack.usage_context: Character, party, and campaign resource storage

conventions: Firestore Security Rules use v2 syntax
Custom functions defined before match blocks in firestore.rules
Firestore operations in db.ts use firestoreTimeout wrapper for protection against hangs
All Firestore writes use try-catch with best-effort fallbacks where appropriate
Party membership tracked BOTH via party doc members map AND members subcollection (legacy)
DM operations (kick, update party name, delete party) guarded by isPartyDM() in rules

hard: Only the DM can update party documents (enforced by firestore rules)
Only the DM can delete party documents (enforced by firestore rules)
Players joining via code must NOT get DM-level permissions
isPartyMember() must work for both DM-joined and code-joined players
npm run build must pass with strict TypeScript
soft: Members subcollection should be the single source of truth for membership
Code changes should be minimal deletions, not rewrites
compatibility: Existing parties with stale members map data are still functional
New parties should not include unnecessary members map field
security_requirements: Players must not be able to modify party documents
Players must be able to read campaign resources for their party
DM retains exclusive write access to party documents

patterns: joinParty flow: find party by code → update character doc → create subcollection member doc → update party members map → broadcast to RTDB
Security rules: isAuth() guard → isPartyDM() for DM operations → isPartyMember() for player resource access
Membership tracked via two mechanisms (party doc map + subcollection) — subcollection is reliable, map was never populated for code joiners

key_components.name: isPartyMember
responsibility: Check if auth user is member of a party for access control

key_components.name: joinParty
responsibility: Join a party by code: find party, update character, create member record

key_components.name: createParty
responsibility: Create new party with DM as owner

key_components.name: removeFromParty
responsibility: Remove character from party (player leave or DM kick)

key_components.name: isPartyDM
responsibility: Check if auth user is the DM of a party

purpose: Firestore security rules defining all access control
why_relevant: isPartyMember() function needs fix; party document update rule causes the bug

key_elements.description: Checks if auth user is party member by reading party doc members map — should check subcollection
Checks if auth user is party DM — used to gate party doc updates
Only DM can update party doc — this is why step 4 fails for joining players
Player can create own member doc — step 3 succeeds
Uses isPartyMember() — currently broken for code joiners
members: {} initialization — no longer needed, remove
setDoc to members subcollection — WORKS, source of truth for membership
Broadcast to RTDB — stays after step 4 removal

patterns_found.name: Best-effort secondary operations
example_location: db.ts:587-596 try-catch on members map cleanup

gotchas.text: Timeouts: Firestore operations getDoc/setDoc/updateDoc can hang indefinitely when Firestore is unreachable. All db.ts operations should use firestoreTimeout() wrapper. T2 removal must preserve the timeout pattern from surrounding code.

Two RTDB listeners: App.tsx (selective path) and SheetTabs (all path) both write to setCharacters(). If a broadcast fires from joinParty, both listeners fire. The existing timestamp guard (incoming > current) prevents loopback.
isPartyMember() is also called from rules for /parties/{partyId}/resources — fixing it unlocks resource access for joined players.

security_considerations.concern: Membership verification must use the subcollection (reliable) not the party doc map (unreliable for code joiners)

domain_context.error_handling: joinParty wraps everything in one try-catch, returning {partyId: null, partyName: null, error: string}. Failed operations throw errors that propagate to the catch block.
domain_context.data_flow: Player enters party code → joinParty() → getDocs parties (find by code) → setDoc characters/{charId} (assign party_id) → setDoc parties/{partyId}/members/{uid} (create member record) → [REMOVED: updateDoc party members map] → broadcastCharacterUpdate (RTDB notification)

open_questions.question: Do any other functions or code paths read the party doc's members map field for functional purposes (not security)?
open_questions.context: If anything reads this field, removing it from createParty could cause undefined access

prior_decisions.decision: isPartyMember() will use exists() on members/{uid} subcollection instead of get() on party doc members map
rationale: Step 3 of joinParty (setDoc to subcollection) already succeeds for all joiners
Step 4 (updateDoc party members map) always fails for non-DM joiners
exists() is same cost (1 read) but semantically cleaner
Makes subcollection the single source of truth for membership
linked_constraints: Players must not get DM-level permissions
isPartyMember() must work for code-joined players

prior_decisions.decision: Remove failing updateDoc from joinParty() entirely (best-effort)
rationale: The updateDoc is the direct cause of the 'Missing or insufficient permissions' error
Membership is already reliably tracked via subcollection
No functional code depends on the party doc members map
Simpler than wrapping in try-catch (would mask the error but subcollection is sufficient)
evidence: firestore.rules:115 allow create: isAuth() && uid() == memberId
linked_constraints: Only the DM can update party documents

prior_decisions.decision: Remove dead members map field from createParty() and dead cleanup from removeFromParty()
rationale: members field on party doc is no longer read by any code or rule
Removing reduces confusion and keeps codebase consistent with subcollection-only model
No migration needed — existing parties with the field are unaffected, new parties won't have it
linked_constraints: New parties should not include unnecessary fields

do_not_re_read: Root cause: updateDoc fails because only DM can update party doc (firestore.rules:106)
isPartyMember() checks party doc members map — fails for code joiners since map is never populated (firestore.rules:35-38)
joinParty step 3 (setDoc to members subcollection) always succeeds (firestore.rules:115)
safe_to_assume: Members subcollection is the reliable source of truth for party membership
The party doc members map was never successfully written for non-DM joiners
No functional code reads the party doc members map for display or logic purposes
verify_before_use: If adding new features that need party membership data, use the members subcollection, NOT the party doc members map
If adding new security rules that check membership, use exists() on members/{uid} subcollection
